provider.name: Coastal Elements AI (with Poly186 delivery team)
provider.contactSummary: Dylan Ryan (Founder / Principal) - Primary business and account lead; Princeps Polycap (Technical Lead, poly186.io) - AI/LLM architecture and implementation; Drew (Delivery Manager) - Project coordination and delivery management
provider.contactNotes: Coastal Elements AI leads the engagement; Poly186 is the delivery partner for technical implementation.

intermediary.name: Richard (referrer)
intermediary.contactSummary: Richard - Broker/associate connected to S.L. Nusbaum; made initial introduction
intermediary.contactNotes: Introduced Coastal Elements AI to S.L. Nusbaum via brokerage relationships.

client.name: S.L. Nusbaum Realty Co.
client.contactSummary: Mark Ashley (Director of IT) - Primary client contact; oversees Azure, 365/Copilot and AI governance for corporate and multifamily portfolio
client.contactNotes: Mark will coordinate internal department discovery and governance review.

scopeDeliverables: Initial AI/automation discovery discussions with IT leadership (completed in this call).
Proposed workshop framework for departmental discovery (HR, Finance/Accounting, Multifamily operations, Brokerage).
Questionnaire/document with discovery questions for departmental meetings to surface AI/automation use cases and KPIs/OKRs.
High-level assessment of existing AI-related tools (current utility-bill bot, LOB AI features, Microsoft Copilot usage).
Identification and prioritization of AI use cases with clear ROI, focusing on process-heavy and documentation-heavy workflows.
Definition of at least one quick win AI/automation use case to demonstrate value and ROI.
Architecture recommendation for deployment in S.L. Nusbaum Azure environment (LLM selection, hosting, security/governance).
Future-state concept/proposal for a larger, client-facing AI-enabled offering S.L. Nusbaum can market.
Optional design/build of AI agents/automations either as tenant-owned Azure solutions or access to Coastal's SaaS offerings where appropriate.
phases: Phase 1 – Discovery & Audit: Understand current environment, constraints, and candidate AI use cases across departments; Activities: Audit existing processes and tooling, Departmental workshops (HR, Finance, Multifamily, Brokerage), Elicit KPIs/OKRs, Assess Azure and Microsoft 365/Copilot setup; Dependencies: Access to Mark and selected department heads, Agreement on governance and security expectations; Outputs: Workshop framework and discovery-question pack, Discovery summary mapped to KPIs/OKRs, Shortlist of high-ROI quick wins

Phase 2 – Prioritization & POC Design: Select and design initial POC(s) with clear success metrics; Activities: Prioritize use cases with Mark, Define POC scope and ROI model, Choose LLMs and tooling, Design Azure hosting architecture; Dependencies: Completion of discovery workshops, Executive alignment and budget, Access to sample data with security controls; Outputs: POC design documents, ROI and business case materials, Implementation plan and estimated effort/cost

Phase 3 – POC Implementation & Evaluation: Implement selected POC(s), measure impact, and prepare for scale; Activities: Build AI agents in Azure or Coastal POC environment, Integrate with systems (SharePoint, LOB apps), User acceptance testing, Measure pre/post metrics, Refine prompts and guardrails; Dependencies: Approved POC scope and budget, Availability of pilot users and sample workloads, Security and compliance review; Outputs: Working POC(s) with measurable ROI, Before/after metrics, Recommendations for productionization

Phase 4 – Productionization & Marketable Offering: Scale successful solutions and shape marketable offerings; Activities: Harden and scale automations, Decide deployment model (tenant-owned vs Coastal SaaS), Craft externally-facing value proposition and collateral, Identify verticalized SaaS opportunities; Dependencies: Successful POC outcomes and executive sign-off, Alignment on product vs bespoke strategy, Ongoing governance model; Outputs: Production-ready workflows, Externally marketable service descriptions, Roadmap for future enhancements
timelineDuration: Exploratory pre-engagement period through at least mid-January; subsequent phases TBD based on January discussions and budgeting.
milestones: Send workshop framework & discovery questions — Within ~1 week after the November 5 meeting: Dylan to email Mark example workshop structure and discovery questions.
Internal departmental conversations by Mark — Nov–Dec (budget season): Mark to meet with executives and department heads to surface AI needs.
Department head meeting — Later in November: Obtain deeper departmental needs and candidate use cases.
Brokers' monthly meeting — January: Mark to gather detailed custom AI/automation asks from brokers.
Mid-January follow-up meeting — Mid-January: Coastal Elements AI and Mark to regroup to review discovery outcomes, prioritize, and discuss POC options.
Informal lunch/beers meeting — Before or around January follow-up: Relationship-building and informal discussion of opportunities.
paymentTerms: POC commercial proposal: Fixed fee for initial POC phase proposed at $25,000 (illustrative). Payment schedule: 50% due upon execution of this Agreement, 40% due upon delivery of working POC, 10% due within 30 days of formal acceptance. Additional consulting beyond POC: $200/hour. Travel and out-of-pocket expenses reimbursed at cost. Invoices due net 30 days. Any change in scope will be documented and mutually agreed with estimates for additional fees.
estimatedValue: POC engagement estimated $20,000 - $35,000 depending on scope; productionization and marketable offering pricing to be estimated after POC.
roiExpectation: Engagements must show clear, quantifiable ROI—primarily via time savings and process improvements in manual/document-heavy workflows—and, for larger investments, contribute to an externally marketable AI-enabled offering that S.L. Nusbaum can present to its clients.
nextSteps: Dylan Ryan / Coastal Elements AI: Schedule mid-January follow-up meeting with Mark to review discovered AI opportunities (Planned).
Dylan Ryan / Coastal Elements AI: Email Mark workshop framework and departmental discovery questions (Planned).
Dylan Ryan / Coastal Elements AI: Consider marketable, client-facing AI offerings lens when drafting workshop materials (Planned).
Mark Ashley: Conduct internal one-on-ones with executives and department heads to identify AI/automation needs (Planned).
Mark Ashley: Attend department head meeting (later this month) and brokers' meeting in January to gather detailed AI requests (Planned).
Dylan Ryan & Mark Ashley: Schedule informal lunch/beers to deepen relationship before formal January follow-up (Planned).
Coastal Elements AI (Princeps & team): Prepare example POC scenarios tailored to S.L. Nusbaum's Azure environment once internal discovery is complete (Pending).
keyQuotes: My goal is to really force the vendors into offering this stuff, rather than getting into the trap that the company had been in for the last 15 to 20 years, where there's a bunch of homebrew, one-off, you know, snowflake solutions.
The amount of time spent by resources doing this versus the amount of time now being spent on that same activity, that shows an ROI there immediately… You don't want to just do AI for the sake of AI.

For the immediate future, I think allowing me some time to really, you know, start having these conversations… Maybe mid-January, kind of regroup, and see what conversations we're starting to have, and see if there's an opportunity, and just go from there.

What you're going to start seeing is companies like ours really starting to market what AI solutions they have and how we're offering AI to benefit our customers. And if we're not doing that, then I think that it's going to pass us by.
providerSignatureName: Dylan Ryan, Founder / Principal, Coastal Elements AI
clientSignatureName: Mark Ashley, Director of IT, S.L. Nusbaum Realty Co.
notes: This Agreement is a pilot/POC engagement framework intended to be followed by detailed SOW(s) and statements of work for subsequent phases as required.
